FFmpeg release: 8.1.2 "Hoare"
Release tag: n8.1.2
Release commit: 38b88335f99e76ed89ff3c93f877fdefce736c13
Build profile revision: 10
Deployment target: macOS 14.0
Architectures: arm64 and x86_64, built separately and merged with lipo
License target: GPL 2.0-or-later
x264 revision: 0480cb05fa188d37ae87e8f4fd8f1aea3711f7ee
x264 source date: 2025-09-10

The two slices use the same component profile. ARCH, SDKROOT, compiler paths,
prefix, and -arch flags are supplied by Scripts/build-ffmpeg.sh.
External package discovery is disabled. A fail-closed pkg-config shim exposes
only the x264 library built from the pinned source inside the stable build
root.

Reproducibility paths:

- Build/source root: `/private/tmp/com.example.Resizer.ffmpeg-build/8.1.2-profile10`
- Recorded virtual prefix: `/opt/resizer-toolchain/ffmpeg/8.1.2-profile10/<arch>`
- Compiler path mapping: the build/source root is mapped to
  `/usr/src/resizer-ffmpeg/8.1.2-profile10`

The fixed build root, virtual prefix, C locale, UTC timezone, deterministic
archive timestamps, and Clang file/debug/macro prefix maps prevent a developer
username or repository checkout path from being embedded in the distributed
binaries. `Scripts/build-ffmpeg.sh` rejects outputs and saved reports that
contain such paths.

Pinned source patch:

Vendor/FFmpeg/patches/0001-avformat-fd-accept-descriptor-in-url.patch
SHA-256: 7f2095aae417027256fa76640aa1bfe44e37cc46bdffea269e8203353930cc5d

The patch preserves the legacy `fd:` behavior and adds strict decimal
`fd:<number>` URLs for nonnegative descriptors through INT_MAX. An explicit URL
descriptor takes precedence over the `fd` protocol option. Signs, whitespace,
trailing characters, and numeric overflow are rejected. On Darwin, every
seekable `fd` context keeps an independent logical offset and uses `pread` /
`pwrite`; streamed descriptors keep the legacy sequential behavior. This avoids
shared-open-file-description offset interference when the MOV muxer reopens an
explicit `fd:<number>` URL during `+faststart`. That descriptor must be opened
for both reading and writing, and should point to an empty regular file. Because
this profile relies on Darwin's positional-I/O semantics, enabling the patched
`fd` protocol for a non-Darwin target fails at compile time.

Pinned x264 source patch:

Vendor/x264/patches/0001-reproducible-version-metadata.patch
SHA-256: 1ed1013a8eedfc99b51d89715efa3176286efbe9200ee0b83d604490cf9f086a

The deterministic x264 archive intentionally contains no Git metadata. This
patch lets the fail-closed build script inject the pinned r3223 and short
commit ID into x264's generated version header, matching a build from the
pinned upstream checkout.

--target-os=darwin
--enable-cross-compile
--pkg-config-flags=--static
--cpu=generic
--disable-autodetect
--disable-everything
--disable-network
--disable-avdevice
--disable-ffplay
--disable-doc
--disable-debug
--disable-shared
--enable-static
--disable-x86asm
--disable-iconv
--disable-audiotoolbox
--enable-videotoolbox
--enable-ffmpeg
--enable-ffprobe
--enable-protocol=fd
--enable-protocol=file
--enable-protocol=pipe
--enable-demuxer=mov
--enable-muxer=mp4
--enable-decoder=h264
--enable-decoder=hevc
--enable-decoder=aac
--enable-parser=h264
--enable-parser=hevc
--enable-parser=aac
--enable-gpl
--enable-libx264
--enable-encoder=libx264
--enable-encoder=hevc_videotoolbox
--enable-encoder=aac
--enable-filter=scale
--enable-filter=aresample
--fatal-warnings

Explicitly absent:

--enable-version3
--enable-nonfree
--enable-libx265

The sole external library is the pinned, statically linked x264 snapshot. The
x264 profile is 8-bit 4:2:0, static-library-only, with PIC enabled and its CLI
and OpenCL disabled. arm64 uses x264 assembly; x86_64 uses the dependency-free C path
because Xcode does not provide NASM. Network support and all network protocols
are disabled. Only the local fd, file, and pipe protocols are enabled. The only
bundled command-line programs are ffmpeg and ffprobe.
